title: Lisa The Greek

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening sequence presumably shows Homer in some mishap
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's catchphrase - iconic character integration and economy, but not original within the show's context

jokes.text: Well, bye-bye belt.
jokes.setup: Sports announcer mentions six hours of football action
jokes.punchline: Homer anticipates eating so much he'll outgrow his belt
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about Homer's eating habits, economical delivery

jokes.text: Homer, all those fatty... deep-fried, heavily-salted snacks... can't be good for your heart. My heart is just fine.
jokes.setup: Marge expresses concern about Homer's unhealthy eating
jokes.punchline: Homer claims his heart is fine while presumably experiencing chest pain
jokes.explanation: Standard ironic denial of obvious health problems, fits character but not particularly inventive

jokes.text: A little beer will put out that fire.
jokes.setup: Homer is experiencing heartburn from unhealthy food
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks alcohol will solve a health problem
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - solving problems with more bad habits, good character integration

jokes.text: the man who's right... 52% of the time, Smooth Jimmy Apollo
jokes.setup: Introduction of a betting expert
jokes.punchline: 52% success rate is barely better than coin flip but presented as expertise
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of sports betting 'experts' - the specific percentage makes it funnier than generic mockery

jokes.text: Moe's Tavern... where the peanut bowl is freshened hourly.
jokes.setup: Standard business phone greeting format
jokes.punchline: Hilariously low bar for tavern hygiene standards
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of business slogan tropes - the 'hourly' detail makes it perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Chief Wiggum, could you hand me... that little black book? Sure thing, Moe. I was using it as a coaster.
jokes.setup: Moe asks the police chief to help with illegal gambling
jokes.punchline: Wiggum casually assists while using evidence as furniture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - helping with crime while treating evidence carelessly

jokes.text: All right, Denver, justify my love.
jokes.setup: Homer has bet on Denver and wants them to win
jokes.punchline: References Madonna song 'Justify My Love' in sports betting context
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference works but feels slightly forced - economy saves it

jokes.text: This is where she prints her feminist newsletter.
jokes.setup: Lisa shows off her doll's apartment
jokes.punchline: 8-year-old's doll has sophisticated political activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - her intelligence and social awareness applied to child's play

jokes.text: things I'll tell you when you're older
jokes.setup: Marge lists ways she connects with Homer's interests
jokes.punchline: Vague reference to sexual activities
jokes.explanation: Standard adult humor in kids' show format, functional but not memorable

jokes.text: Just don't say anything, and sit down over there. Over.
jokes.setup: Lisa wants to watch football with Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer gives military-style orders to his daughter, ending with 'Over'
jokes.explanation: Homer's awkward attempt at father-daughter bonding, the 'Over' adds military absurdity

jokes.text: See you in hell, soldier boy.
jokes.setup: Denver fumbles in the game
jokes.punchline: Homer's disproportionately hostile response to sports disappointment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's sports rage, economical and character-appropriate

jokes.text: Even though I'd love to spend... this sunny afternoon trying on clothes, it's not...
jokes.setup: Marge suggests clothes shopping
jokes.punchline: Bart's sarcastic agreement is cut short by visual of his horrible clothes
jokes.explanation: Visual gag setup - Bart's sarcasm interrupted by the evidence he needs new clothes

jokes.text: you're wrong 48% of the time. Why didn't you say that before?!
jokes.setup: Jimmy admits his failure rate
jokes.punchline: Homer outraged by obvious mathematical complement
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier joke, Homer's mathematical incompetence is perfectly in character

jokes.text: by 200 points?! Why, you worthless hunk of junk!
jokes.setup: Computer predicts Cincinnati will win
jokes.punchline: Impossible 200-point margin in football, Homer destroys machine
jokes.explanation: Absurd score prediction makes technology seem incompetent, Homer's violent reaction is character-appropriate

jokes.text: Don't you realize this is costing me money?
jokes.setup: Coach continues speaking painfully slowly
jokes.punchline: Homer's desperate plea falls on deaf ears
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the hotline frustration, Homer's helplessness is relatable

jokes.text: The Dolphins? Good, good.
jokes.setup: Homer asks Lisa for a random pick
jokes.punchline: Homer treats 8-year-old's guess as expert advice
jokes.explanation: Sets up the main plot device - Homer's desperation leading to trusting Lisa

jokes.text: That's because people who wear them get beaten up.
jokes.setup: Marge points out a shirt is 50% off
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact explanation for the discount
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery, subverts retail logic with brutal honesty

jokes.text: Well, anyone who would do that isn't your friend.
jokes.setup: Bart explains people get beaten up for wearing certain clothes
jokes.punchline: Marge's naive maternal response to bullying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - maternal optimism completely missing the point about bullies

jokes.text: How did you know?
jokes.setup: Marge tells Bart not to make 'that face'
jokes.punchline: Marge somehow knows Bart is making a face she can't see
jokes.explanation: Classic mother intuition joke, economical and relatable

jokes.text: It's a little thing daddies do... to make football more exciting.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to explain gambling to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Minimizes illegal gambling as innocent father behavior
jokes.explanation: Homer's poor parenting on display, trying to normalize gambling through euphemism

jokes.text: Mmm... crumbled-up cookie things.
jokes.setup: Homer uses ice cream sundae analogy for gambling
jokes.punchline: Lisa gets distracted by food description, missing the gambling lesson
jokes.explanation: Callback to Homer's food reactions, shows Lisa's still a kid despite intelligence

jokes.text: My God... it's like there's some kind of bond between us.
jokes.setup: Lisa agrees gambling makes things better
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks shared vice creates father-daughter connection
jokes.explanation: Homer's twisted logic about bonding through gambling, sets up episode's emotional core

jokes.text: We've got a troublemaker in booth eight. There's a code red in booth three.
jokes.setup: Department store clothing section
jokes.punchline: Military-level security response to clothing shopping
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - treating clothing store like military operation

jokes.text: My God! Those aren't the socks... she came in with.
jokes.setup: Security monitoring the fitting rooms
jokes.punchline: Dramatic discovery that Marge changed Bart's socks
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the security paranoia - treating sock change like major crime

jokes.text: You could use some new underwear too.
jokes.setup: Bart is presumably naked or undressed in fitting room
jokes.punchline: Marge's observation about his underwear condition
jokes.explanation: Mild embarrassment humor, functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: somewhere in the back
jokes.setup: Lisa asks where the Bible says gambling is okay
jokes.punchline: Homer's vague, obviously false biblical reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer BS - vague enough to sound plausible to a kid, obviously wrong to adults

jokes.text: You bet.
jokes.setup: Marge asks if they had fun
jokes.punchline: Homer's response is literal gambling pun
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pun - Homer can't help but reference betting even in casual conversation

jokes.text: You got to come out sometime, Simpson.
jokes.setup: Bart is hiding in fitting room wearing new clothes
jokes.punchline: Bullies waiting outside to mock his outfit
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier clothing violence joke, Bart's fashion fears realized

jokes.text: It gets rid... of the unpleasant aftertaste of church.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks to watch football again next Sunday
jokes.punchline: Homer describes church as having an 'unpleasant aftertaste'
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor - treating church like bad medicine that needs a chaser

jokes.text: Dad tucked me in too tight, and it's cutting off... the circulation in my arms and legs.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks Marge to loosen her blanket
jokes.punchline: Homer's over-enthusiastic tucking causes medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy about Homer's lack of restraint, even in gentle activities

jokes.text: The fear in his eyes, the quiver in his voice. He's a little boy lost in a game of men.
jokes.setup: Lisa analyzes a player's pre-game interview
jokes.punchline: 8-year-old provides sophisticated psychological analysis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - adult-level insight from a child, establishes her 'gift'

jokes.text: I'd bet my entire college fund on it.
jokes.setup: Lisa gives confident prediction
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions betting away her education fund
jokes.explanation: Dark irony - brilliant child willing to gamble away her future education

jokes.text: The 49ers because they're pure of heart... Seattle because they got something to prove... and the Raiders because they cheat.
jokes.setup: Homer asks for afternoon game picks
jokes.punchline: Lisa's reasoning goes from noble to cynical
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from idealistic to pragmatic reasoning, great character work

jokes.text: It doesn't take a genius... to realize Houston's failed to cover their last ten outings... the week after scoring three touchdowns... in a game.
jokes.setup: Lisa explains her successful pick
jokes.punchline: Complex statistical analysis from 8-year-old
jokes.explanation: Great Lisa characterization - dismisses her genius while demonstrating it

jokes.text: My little girl says the cutest things.
jokes.setup: Lisa gives complex gambling analysis
jokes.punchline: Homer treats sophisticated reasoning as cute child talk
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - completely missing the sophistication of Lisa's analysis

jokes.text: Not Sunday-- Daddy-Daughter Day.
jokes.setup: Lisa says Sunday is becoming her favorite day
jokes.punchline: Homer rebrands it as their special day
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment that sets up the emotional stakes - Homer thinks it's about bonding

jokes.text: you lucky moron
jokes.setup: Homer keeps winning bets
jokes.punchline: Moe's backhanded acknowledgment of Homer's luck
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe - grudging respect mixed with insult

jokes.text: I used to hate the smell of your sweaty feet. Now it's the smell of victory.
jokes.setup: Homer continues winning
jokes.punchline: Moe's attitude toward Homer's hygiene changes with money
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for Moe - money changes his perception of everything

jokes.text: I'm Homer, I'll be your customer.
jokes.setup: Waiter introduces himself formally
jokes.punchline: Homer mirrors the introduction literally
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay - Homer's literal interpretation of social conventions

jokes.text: Never heard that one before.
jokes.setup: Homer makes his customer joke
jokes.punchline: Waiter's sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic response, functional but not memorable

jokes.text: Just bring us your freshest bottle of wine. Chop, chop.
jokes.setup: Lisa takes over wine ordering
jokes.punchline: 8-year-old acts like sophisticated wine snob
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Lisa's precociousness applied to inappropriate situations

jokes.text: ''The Broken Neck Blues.''
jokes.setup: Homer asks Lisa's favorite song for violin player
jokes.punchline: Lisa requests morbid song title
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny song title, shows Lisa's sophisticated but morbid humor

jokes.text: Maybe the paint has shut off his pores... and he's slowly suffocating. That is a real fan.
jokes.setup: Lisa observes painted fan at cold football game
jokes.punchline: Casual speculation about fan's potential death
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - Lisa's morbid fascination with sports dedication

jokes.text: You call Moe. Just bet, Lisa.
jokes.setup: Lisa gives complex betting advice
jokes.punchline: Homer reduces 8-year-old to gambling tool
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's exploitation escalating - treating Lisa like gambling machine

jokes.text: When the doctor said... I didn't have worms anymore, that was the happiest day... of my life.
jokes.setup: Class assignment about happiest day
jokes.punchline: Ralph's medical overshare
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph Wiggum moment - inappropriate oversharing in innocent context

jokes.text: when the Saints-- four and a half point favorites... only up by three-- kicked a meaningless field goal... at the last second to cover the spread.
jokes.setup: Lisa's essay about her happiest day
jokes.punchline: 8-year-old's happiness tied to gambling technicalities
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy - innocent school assignment reveals gambling corruption

jokes.text: Dear God!
jokes.setup: Lisa's gambling essay
jokes.punchline: Teacher's horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat to Lisa's inappropriate essay, economical shock

jokes.text: '' Meryl Streep's Versatility.''
jokes.setup: Lisa discovers perfume
jokes.punchline: Absurd celebrity perfume name
jokes.explanation: Creative celebrity perfume parody - 'versatility' as fragrance concept is absurd

jokes.text: Shut up! Shut up! Kiss my butt! Go to hell!
jokes.setup: Lisa receives a gift
jokes.punchline: Children's toy with inappropriately hostile phrases
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of inappropriate toy sayings, becomes more hostile

jokes.text: Dad, I promise... I will never get tired of this.
jokes.setup: Lisa enjoys her gambling-bought gifts
jokes.punchline: Promise that will obviously be broken
jokes.explanation: Dramatic irony - audience knows this happiness won't last

jokes.text: Only in 48 states.
jokes.setup: Marge says gambling is illegal
jokes.punchline: Homer's technically correct but missing-the-point response
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - technically accurate but morally oblivious

jokes.text: The only victim is Moe... and it's brought Lisa and me together.
jokes.setup: Homer claims gambling is victimless
jokes.punchline: Immediately identifies a victim, then ignores it for selfish reason
jokes.explanation: Great Homer reasoning - contradicts himself immediately then justifies with emotion

jokes.text: The new football season is only seven months away.
jokes.setup: Lisa wants to spend time together after Super Bowl
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses her until gambling returns
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking Homer selfishness - only values Lisa for gambling utility

jokes.text: You never wanted to be with me at all. You're a very selfish man.
jokes.setup: Lisa realizes Homer's true motivation
jokes.punchline: 8-year-old delivers devastating character assessment
jokes.explanation: Emotional climax - Lisa's mature insight exposes Homer's immaturity

jokes.text: Go to hell! Go to hell! Hey, once again, great present, Dad.
jokes.setup: Tense emotional moment between Lisa and Homer
jokes.punchline: Toy's hostility contrasts with Lisa's sarcastic gratitude
jokes.explanation: Good callback - toy's profanity punctuates the emotional moment

jokes.text: I've been gambling since I was eight... and hocking jewelry since I was 1 2.
jokes.setup: Woman pawns jewelry at casino
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of lifelong gambling addiction starting in childhood
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about gambling addiction - the escalating timeline is effectively disturbing

jokes.text: All this was bought with dirty money. Your penthouse, your Alfa Romeo, your collagen-injection clinic.
jokes.setup: Lisa addresses her Malibu Stacy doll
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly sophisticated possessions for children's toy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - doll's lifestyle gets more adult and materialistic

jokes.text: However, I may also be so clouded with rage... that subconsciously... I want you to lose. In which case, I'd bet the farm on Buffalo.
jokes.setup: Lisa explains her conflicted feelings
jokes.punchline: 8-year-old's sophisticated psychological self-analysis
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lisa characterization - adult-level emotional intelligence applied to gambling

jokes.text: If I still love you... Washington. If I don't, Buffalo.
jokes.setup: Lisa's emotional conflict about helping Homer
jokes.punchline: Reduces father-daughter relationship to betting formula
jokes.explanation: Brilliant emotional climax - love quantified through gambling, perfectly crafted

jokes.text: Every note is a dagger in my heart. I got to get out of here.
jokes.setup: National anthem plays at Super Bowl
jokes.punchline: Homer can't handle emotional pressure of the game
jokes.explanation: Homer's melodrama about sports anxiety, reasonable character moment

jokes.text: I play Jack Handle, a retired cop... who shares an apartment with a retired criminal. The original odd couple.
jokes.setup: Troy describes his new sitcom
jokes.punchline: 'Handle' pun and generic TV premise
jokes.explanation: Standard Troy McClure bit - bad pun and cliched premise

jokes.text: and my recent trouble with the I RS sealed the deal.
jokes.setup: Troy explains why he took the TV job
jokes.punchline: Admits financial desperation on live TV
jokes.explanation: Troy's typical oversharing - inappropriate honesty about career motivations

jokes.text: People of Earth, we've come ten billion light-years... to bring you this halftime message of peace.
jokes.setup: Super Bowl halftime show
jokes.punchline: Aliens travel impossible distance for banal halftime entertainment
jokes.explanation: Absurdist Super Bowl halftime parody - cosmic effort for mundane message

jokes.text: Oh, this sucks. Come on, snipers, where are you?
jokes.setup: Boring halftime show
jokes.punchline: Bart wishes for violent interruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - casual violence as entertainment solution

jokes.text: You-Hate-Dad by a touchdown.
jokes.setup: Marge asks who's winning
jokes.punchline: Bart treats Lisa's anger as a team score
jokes.explanation: Brilliant joke - sports metaphor for family dynamics, perfect economy and originality

jokes.text: They wanted it more.
jokes.setup: Duff Dry wins some competition
jokes.punchline: Cliched sports analysis applied to beer commercial contest
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of sports commentary cliches applied to commercial context

jokes.text: If Washington scores, happy fans will be looting... and turning over cars in our nation's capital tonight.
jokes.setup: Final moments of Super Bowl
jokes.punchline: Announcer matter-of-factly predicts victory riots
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - casual prediction of celebratory violence

jokes.text: Money comes and goes, but my daughter and I... can go on for eight more years!
jokes.setup: Homer claims he values relationship over money
jokes.punchline: Immediately calculates exact duration until Lisa grows up
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Homer's attempt at sentiment immediately undercut by calculation

tmdb_overview: Lisa's ability to pick winning football teams kicks off a strong bond with Homer.
insight: Sports obsession escalates through 69 jokes, but emotional plot undercuts the comedic momentum midway through.
